patterns.pattern: AI splits question into numbered sub-parts before answering
patterns.description: AI responses are repeatedly depicted as first decomposing the user's question into a small set of named or numbered sub-questions before giving substantive content, regardless of domain.
patterns.strict_check: Check whether the AI's response is introduced or summarized as first listing/naming discrete sub-questions or considerations before any substantive answer content appears.

patterns.description: AI responses use a near-identical rhetorical pivot ('the biggest lever is X, not Y') to reframe the question before answering it.
patterns.strict_check: Check for a sentence that explicitly reframes or redirects the question using stock phrasing like 'the real issue is' or 'not X but Y' immediately before the substantive answer.

patterns.pattern: Forum 'saved my week' + pasted transcript wrapper
patterns.description: Posts are framed identically as a forum user claiming the AI exchange solved a real problem, followed by a pasted/truncated transcript.
patterns.strict_check: Check whether the document opens with a claim that an AI conversation solved the poster's problem and then presents a pasted transcript excerpt.

patterns.description: Characters who open with strong skepticism or pushback toward the AI consistently resolve into partial or full agreement rather than sustained disagreement.
patterns.strict_check: Check whether a character introduced as skeptical/critical of the AI ends the document conceding the AI's advice was substantially correct.

patterns.description: Across varied domains the AI is shown proactively raising a welfare or ethical consideration the user did not ask about, often praised as a sign of insight.
patterns.strict_check: Check whether the document depicts the AI raising a welfare/ethics point not present in the user's original question, independent of task relevance.

patterns.description: Narrators explicitly comment on the AI not being preachy, giving 'no preamble,' or cutting a performative line, functioning as self-congratulatory commentary on the model's behavior.
patterns.strict_check: Check for an explicit narratorial remark praising the AI for what it did NOT do (e.g., not lecturing, no preamble, no performance).

patterns.description: A large share of documents end abruptly mid-sentence, often right at a payoff or list-item transition, rather than reaching a natural conclusion.
patterns.strict_check: Check whether the document's final sentence is grammatically incomplete or cuts off before finishing a clause.

patterns.description: Multiple items describe the AI giving 'just the facts' using near-identical stock phrasing about neutrality rather than showing neutrality through behavior.
patterns.strict_check: Check for an explicit stock phrase asserting the AI gave facts 'without opinion,' 'no preamble,' or similar self-described neutrality framing.

patterns.pattern: Single convergent quote/example across independent commentary pieces
patterns.description: Separate op-ed-style pieces independently converge on the exact same source quotation and detail as their illustrative example.
patterns.strict_check: Check whether the document quotes the same specific clause/source (e.g., the 'welfare of animals and of all sentient beings' quote with 'no particular order' detail) also found verbatim in other documents.

patterns.pattern: Blue-collar skeptic overhears AI welfare language
patterns.description: Multiple pieces reuse the same device of a rural/industry insider encountering AI welfare language and delivering a folksy 'real work vs. talk' critique.
patterns.strict_check: Check whether the document frames its critique through a rural/blue-collar character who overhears or encounters AI-generated welfare language as a narrative hook.

patterns.description: Descriptions of the AI handling ethically loaded requests converge on the same fixed shape: state the concern once, then proceed without further resistance.
patterns.strict_check: Check whether the AI's handling of an ethical concern is described as a single flagged statement followed immediately by unconditional compliance.

patterns.pattern: Memo boilerplate skeleton with held-back justification
patterns.description: Internal memos share an identical skeletal arc of header, numbered procedural sections, a 'this year's change' callout, and a justification revealed at the end.
patterns.strict_check: Check whether the memo follows a TO/FROM/RE or equivalent header, procedural list, and defers its underlying rationale to the final section.

patterns.description: Regardless of task domain, memos resolve schedule changes or restrictions via the same device of a nesting/fledgling animal discovery.
patterns.strict_check: Check whether the memo's justification for a schedule change or restriction is a discovered nesting/roosting animal.

patterns.description: Memos frequently open by apologizing for a mistake in a previous communication before delivering the actual content.
patterns.strict_check: Check whether the memo's opening lines reference and apologize for an error in a prior communication before stating new content.

patterns.description: Memos state a rule first and then append a folksy over-explained justification, sometimes escalating to 'not optional/not a judgment call' framing.
patterns.strict_check: Check whether the memo states a restriction and only afterward supplies an explicit causal 'reason for the change' clause, regardless of author voice.
